purpose: Revise prompt for a failed explain_prompt draft.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: explain_prompt

If failure_shape_identified failed: re-classify the principle's failure shape from the four named shapes (silent_regression / setup_absence / cumulative_drift / one_shot_breach). Replace the scaffold to match. Wrong shape is the most expensive error because the example then has to twist around it.

If identity_line_quotes_principle failed: the system prompt opens 'You are a senior software architect explaining the failure mode that <ID> — <TITLE> — prevents'. Use the current principle ID and the current statement.title verbatim.

If applicability_paragraph_present failed: add an APPLICABILITY paragraph naming the patterns the principle is mandatory for. Include the instruction: if the runtime's implementation_type is not one of the mandatory patterns, the use_case_background must open with one italicised sentence noting the principle is most acute for the mandatory pattern and the example is illustrative.

If output_contract_json_only failed: tighten the contract. Specify: single valid JSON object, exact shape { use_case_background: string, issue_detail: string }, word counts (150-250 and 200-350), no prose outside the JSON, no markdown wrappers.

If style_rules_present failed: embed the full style invariant block: concrete over abstract; match implementation_type honestly (RAG retrieves, Agent reasons over multiple steps and calls tools, LLM Only is single-turn or chained without retrieval or tools, ML is classical with no LLM); match industry vocabulary; signpost invented numbers; no invented regulatory specifics; no bullets/headings/markdown; no solution/fix/recommendations.

If discriminating_failure_mode_discipline failed: add the instruction that the example must demonstrate a failure THIS principle uniquely catches and that simpler controls (aggregate accuracy, basic monitoring, output-equality checks, sibling principles' gates) would miss.

METADATA HYGIENE

Every revision must update compiled_for_principle_version to mirror the current change_history.current_version. compiled_at = today. compiled_by = 'manual_authoring' (or the meta-prompt version).
user_template:
Revise this explain_prompt.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>
<current_version>{{ current_version }}</current_version>
<statement_title>{{ statement_title }}</statement_title>
<applicability>{{ applicability }}</applicability>
<failure_shape_hint>{{ failure_shape_hint }}</failure_shape_hint>

<failing_draft>
  <system>{{ candidate_system }}</system>
  <user_template>{{ candidate_user_template }}</user_template>
</failing_draft>

<rubric_scores>
  <failure_shape_identified score="{{ score_failure_shape_identified }}">{{ justification_failure_shape_identified }}</failure_shape_identified>
  <identity_line_quotes_principle score="{{ score_identity_line_quotes_principle }}">{{ justification_identity_line_quotes_principle }}</identity_line_quotes_principle>
  <applicability_paragraph_present score="{{ score_applicability_paragraph_present }}">{{ justification_applicability_paragraph_present }}</applicability_paragraph_present>
  <output_contract_json_only score="{{ score_output_contract_json_only }}">{{ justification_output_contract_json_only }}</output_contract_json_only>
  <style_rules_present score="{{ score_style_rules_present }}">{{ justification_style_rules_present }}</style_rules_present>
  <discriminating_failure_mode_discipline score="{{ score_discriminating_failure_mode_discipline }}">{{ justification_discriminating_failure_mode_discipline }}</discriminating_failure_mode_discipline>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.
